2026-02-20.name: Improving Emergency Network Reliability with Semantic Voice Perception
2026-02-20.description: This episode explores a major shift toward specialized speech technology, focusing on the paper "Voice-Driven Semantic Perception for UAV-Assisted Emergency Networks." We discuss how semantic communication prioritizes intent over raw audio bits to improve reliability in disaster zones. The conversation also highlights the democratization of the edge with new model releases like the Moonshine-base ONNX variant and sauti-whisper for Swahili, enabling high-performance ASR on local hardware without

2026-02-19.name: Democratizing Speech Technology Through Community Driven Foundation Model Adaptation
2026-02-19.description: This episode explores how community contributors are democratizing speech technology by adapting foundation models for underrepresented languages. We discuss Vitthal Bhandari’s recent MMS-1b fine-tunes for African languages like Meh and Koo, alongside DewiBrynJones’s iterative improvements to Whisper-large-v2 for Welsh speakers. Sam and Alex also dive into specialized tools like faster-whisper-tilsync, highlighting a shift toward high-efficiency models that prioritize timestamp accuracy and regi

2026-02-18.name: Unlocking Efficient Spoken Language Modeling Through Unsupervised Syllable Tokenization
2026-02-18.description: Explore the breakthrough ZeroSyl paper by Visser and Malan, which introduces zero-resource syllable tokenization to significantly reduce sequence lengths in spoken language modeling. We discuss the practical impact of NVIDIA’s Nemotron-Speech 0.6B model being optimized for edge devices through MLX quantization. From specialized IPA-based XLS-R models to fine-tuned MMS architectures for languages like Fongbe, learn how the industry is shifting toward efficient, localized, and linguistically inspi

2026-02-17.name: Advancing Realtime Performance With Unified Multimodal Speech Architectures
2026-02-17.description: This episode explores the release of Mistral AI’s Voxtral-Mini-4B-Realtime, a 4-billion parameter model that integrates audio and language into a single unified space for low-latency edge deployment. We also examine groundbreaking research on CLAP-based automatic word naming for patients with post-stroke aphasia, which replaces traditional phonetic decoding with semantic-acoustic retrieval. The discussion highlights a growing industry trend of moving beyond simple transcription toward direct mul

2026-02-16.name: Bridging the Gap in Speech Recognition Through Targeted Adaptation
2026-02-16.description: We explore the evolution of ASR for low-resource languages by analyzing the massive growth of the Pashto Common Voice dataset alongside a specialized corpus for disordered Akan speech. The discussion highlights the DAMA framework’s efficient U-shaped adaptation strategy and NVIDIA’s Parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v2 model as essential tools for the industry’s shift toward inclusive, local-first speech technology. These insights provide a roadmap for balancing data scale with contributor diversity to create m

2026-02-22.name: Moving Beyond Cascades Toward Native Speech Language Models
2026-02-22.description: This episode explores the industry shift from traditional cascading pipelines to unified Speech-Language Models like Sonic-7B and Whisper-v5. We discuss how discrete neural audio codecs and architectures like Mamba are enabling sub-200 millisecond latency for real-time conversational AI. The conversation also covers the importance of Semantic-Acoustic Decoupling to preserve emotional context and the rise of personalized on-device adaptation using LoRA.

2026-02-21.name: The Growing Dominance of Specialized Precision Speech Models
2026-02-21.description: This episode explores the industry shift from massive foundational models toward hyper-localized and domain-specific tools for speech technology. We analyze the breakthrough Qwen3-ASR-0.6B-Albanian model and how small-scale architectures are outperforming general-purpose giants in low-resource language tasks. Additionally, the conversation covers the whisper-large-v3-Tarteel-even-g2 release for religious recitation and why ONNX optimization in models like medasr-onnx is essential for private, ed

2026-W08.name: Deploying Efficient Multimodal Foundation Models for Realtime Speech Understanding
2026-W08.description: This episode explores the transition from transcription to native audio processing using Mistral AI's Voxtral-Mini-4B-Realtime-2602. We dive into the CLAP-based framework for clinical speech recognition in post-stroke aphasia and discuss the scaling of the Pashto Common Voice dataset. Learn how Depth-Aware Model Adaptation (DAMA) allows for efficient specialization across low-resource languages with significantly fewer parameters.

qwen3-asr-technical-report-deep-dive.name: Scaling Multilingual Speech Recognition with Qwen3-ASR Foundation Models
qwen3-asr-technical-report-deep-dive.description: In this episode, we explore the Qwen3-ASR technical report which introduces a powerful family of models including the Qwen3-ASR-1.7B and a unique non-autoregressive forced aligner. We discuss how the AuT encoder and Group Sequence Policy Optimization allow these models to handle extreme noise, 22 Chinese dialects, and even singing voice recognition with high efficiency. Discover how Alibaba is bridging the gap between frontier performance and on-device deployment using large audio-language model

qwen3-omni-technical-report-deep-dive.name: Scaling Multimodal ASR Performance with the Qwen3-Omni Model
qwen3-omni-technical-report-deep-dive.description: This episode explores the Qwen3-Omni technical report, a landmark release from Alibaba that introduces a multimodal model capable of handling text, audio, and video without performance degradation. We discuss the Thinker-Talker architecture and the AuT encoder, which was trained on an unprecedented 20 million hours of audio data to achieve state-of-the-art results. Discover how the Code2Wav convolutional network enables human-like conversational latency and why the Thinking variant is a game-cha

robust-speech-recognition-via-large-scal.name: Redefining Robustness with Large Scale Weak Supervision in Whisper
robust-speech-recognition-via-large-scal.description: This episode explores the landmark paper Robust Speech Recognition via Large-Scale Weak Supervision which introduced the Whisper model to the world. We discuss how OpenAI moved beyond traditional benchmarks like LibriSpeech by training on 680,000 hours of multilingual data to achieve zero-shot generalization. The conversation covers the transition from self-supervised models like wav2vec 2.0 to multitask architectures that handle transcription, translation, and voice activity detection in one sy

hubert-self-supervised-speech-representa.name: Discretizing Continuous Audio for Self Supervised Speech Learning
hubert-self-supervised-speech-representa.description: We explore the HuBERT paper from Meta, a landmark study that introduced self-supervised speech representation learning through masked prediction of hidden units. The episode breaks down how the researchers used K-means clustering to turn continuous waveforms into discrete tokens, allowing for a BERT-like training objective that surpasses contrastive methods like wav2vec 2.0. Join us as we discuss the iterative re-clustering process and the impressive performance gains of the one-billion paramete

tac-timestamped-audio-captioning-deep-di.name: Grounding Audio Language Models with Precise Temporal Descriptions
tac-timestamped-audio-captioning-deep-di.description: We explore Adobe Research's new TAC model, which introduces timestamped audio captioning to solve the problem of semantic collapse in large audio language models. The guest explains how the Dynamic Acoustic Mixer generates synthetic training data to reduce hallucination rates compared to models like Audio Flamingo 3 and Qwen3-Omni. We also discuss the describe-then-reason paradigm and the TAC-V variant that achieves state-of-the-art results on video benchmarks like DailyOmni.

2026-02-23.name: Scaling Parakeet Token and Duration Transducers for Mobile Hardware
2026-02-23.description: This episode explores the transition of high-end research architectures like NVIDIA’s Parakeet-TDT into the world of edge computing via new CoreML optimizations. We examine how specialized models like ipa-whisper-medium are revolutionizing phonetic transcription for language learning while multimodal releases like Voxtral-Mini-4B integrate speech directly into language models. Discover how these advancements in low-resource language support and cultural rhythm LoRAs are shaping the future of voi

csm-crossing-the-uncanny-valley-of-conve.name: Crossing the Uncanny Valley with Conversational Speech Models
csm-crossing-the-uncanny-valley-of-conve.description: This episode explores the groundbreaking Conversational Speech Model (CSM) from Sesame Research, a new architecture designed to cross the uncanny valley of digital voice. We discuss how the team utilizes the Mimi codec and a Llama-based backbone to generate speech that captures the nuanced social cues and emotional context often lost in traditional cascaded systems. Discover how technical innovations like compute amortization allow this 8-billion parameter model to learn from over a million hour

benchmark-datasets-deep-dive.name: ASR Benchmark Deep Dive: Choosing Models by Dataset, Not Averages
benchmark-datasets-deep-dive.description: A benchmark-first technical podcast episode covering what each major ASR dataset measures and how model rankings shift across LibriSpeech, Common Voice, VoxPopuli, TED-LIUM, GigaSpeech, SPGISpeech, Earnings22, and AMI. Includes practical guidance for selecting models for meeting transcription, finance, and open-domain workloads.

2026-02-24.name: Advancing Real Time Speech Synthesis with Dual Streaming CTC Frameworks
2026-02-24.description: This episode explores the transition from massive model scaling to efficient, real-world deployments using innovative architectures. We dive into CTC-TTS for low-latency dual-streaming audio generation and examine how Matryoshka Representation Learning bridges the digital divide for languages like Wolof. The discussion also covers the surge of edge-ready models on Hugging Face, including the Qwen3-ASR-0.6B and the quantized Kitten-TTS family for local-first voice assistants.

2026-02-25.name: Scaling Low-Resource Speech Recognition Using Cross-Modal Supervision Strategies
2026-02-25.description: This episode explores innovative approaches to automatic speech recognition for low-resource languages like Taiwanese Hokkien and Nepali. We examine how the TG-ASR framework utilizes translation-guided learning and why punctuation restoration modules are critical for robust speech-to-text translation pipelines. The discussion also highlights scaling multimodal large language models via speech-text fusion and the latest community fine-tunes of the Whisper-large-v3 model.

2026-03-09.name: Advancing Specialized Speech Models for Regional Languages and Edge Efficiency
2026-03-09.description: This deep dive explores the shift toward precision speech technology, highlighting the MSP-Visual-AV-HuBERT release for noise-immune audio-visual processing. We examine how the AfriSpeech dataset is empowering local developers through specialized Whisper fine-tuning while discussing the importance of ONNX and MLX quantization for edge deployment. Learn how these breakthroughs in diarization and low-parameter TTS models are closing the deployment gap to create a more accessible, privacy-first glo

2026-03-11.name: Achieving Contextual Audio Understanding through Specialized Reasoning Benchmarks
2026-03-11.description: This episode explores the transition toward Omni-Speech systems that integrate environmental reasoning with traditional transcription capabilities. We discuss how the ALARM framework improves audio-language alignment for reasoning models and why the SCENEBench benchmark is essential for testing real-world acoustic awareness. Additionally, we examine the SPAR-K strategy for reducing inference latency alongside new efficient models like Qwen3-ASR and specialized Whisper fine-tunes.

2026-03-13.name: Scaling Unified Streaming Architectures for Enhanced Paralinguistic Awareness
2026-03-13.description: This report explores the transition toward sophisticated speech intelligence, highlighting the Uni-ASR framework for unified streaming and the Hikari model for policy-free simultaneous translation. Researchers examine breakthroughs in paralinguistic awareness via PE-FT and specialized ASR for Huntington’s disease using clinical biomarkers to improve diagnostic accessibility. These innovations make audio AI more human-centric and efficient across diverse languages and complex clinical conditions.

2026-03-14.name: Surgicalizing Speech Models for Targeted Domain and Style Performance
2026-03-14.description: This episode explores the industry shift from foundational models toward surgicalized, domain-specific speech technology. We analyze the technical nuances of FastConformer for Quranic recitation and the iterative release of thedal-tamil models for South Asian markets. From Whisper Turbo inference optimizations to the rise of Qwen3-TTS variants for accessible voice cloning, learn how practitioners are currently mastering the last mile of speech AI deployment.

2026-03-16.name: Bridging the Gap Between Foundation Models and Localized Utility
2026-03-16.description: This episode explores the industry's shift toward practical localization and edge deployment, moving away from theoretical research to focus on shipping optimized code. We discuss the release of whisper-large-v3-turbo-ko for Korean speech and the iterative development of Tunisian Arabic models by itsarunendra. The conversation also covers the importance of ONNX exports like gigaam-v3-e2e-rnnt for streaming ASR and how ultra-small models like Canary-180m are revolutionizing low-latency applicatio

2026-03-18.name: Bridging Thousands of Languages with Omnilingual SONAR Embeddings
2026-03-18.description: This episode explores massive breakthroughs in cross-lingual representation with the Omnilingual SONAR project, which bridges thousands of languages and modalities into a single semantic space. We also dive into cost-effective localized ASR with Polyglot-Lion, a model fine-tuned for Singapore’s linguistic landscape for under one hundred dollars. Finally, the discussion covers the RECOVER framework for agentic entity correction and the challenges of measuring emotion in synthesized speech.

2026-03-19.name: Advancing Transparency with Verifiable Audio Reasoning Chains
2026-03-19.description: This episode explores the transition toward transparent speech AI through TalTech’s multi-source evidence fusion strategy for verifiable audio reasoning. We discuss Zipper-LoRA’s dynamic parameter decoupling for multilingual ASR and the discovery of emotion-sensitive neurons that enable training-free steering in models like Qwen2.5-Omni. Our roundup concludes with localized model releases for Egyptian Arabic and Kazakh, highlighting the move toward architectural transparency.

2026-03-20.name: Uncovering Latent Auditory Knowledge in Large Audio Language Models
2026-03-20.description: This episode explores the transition from simple transcription to sophisticated audio intelligence through Large Audio Language Models. We examine how latent auditory knowledge in backbones like Qwen impacts performance and discuss the APUN-Bench for measuring phonetic pun understanding. Additionally, we analyze a study on Alzheimer’s disease detection using the ADReSSo 2021 dataset, highlighting how ASR quality affects clinical screening accuracy.

2026-03-26.name: Building Resilient Speech Systems Through Contextual Awareness and Inclusion
2026-03-26.description: This episode explores the transition from raw model scaling to the industrialization of resilient and context-aware speech AI. We discuss how the ACAVCaps dataset enables fine-grained audio scene descriptions and examine methods for mitigating contextual exposure bias using Whisper large-v3 and DPO. Additionally, we highlight the Ethio-ASR suite’s success in low-resource language modeling and the importance of sociolinguistic audits for addressing dialectal bias in modern systems.

2026-04-03.name: Engineering High Performance Speech Models for Clinical and Forensic Use
2026-04-03.description: This episode explores the shift from massive decoder-only models to specialized architectures designed for stability and real-world utility. We dive into the T5Gemma-TTS report and its use of progress-monitoring embeddings for long-form audio, alongside the EndoASR framework’s impressive performance in clinical environments. Finally, the discussion covers the TRACE framework for training-free deepfake detection and how community-driven fine-tuning is bringing high-quality speech tools to low-res

2026-04-05.name: Transitioning From Theoretical Speech Research to Practical Edge Deployment
2026-04-05.description: This episode explores the transition from theoretical research to practical edge deployment by examining how models like Whisper-3-turbo and Parakeet-EOU-120M are being optimized for mobile hardware. We discuss the significance of INT8 quantization for on-device performance and the role of XLS-R in bringing ASR support to low-resource languages like Bemba and Ngiemboon. Learn how the MLX-community and CoreML are enabling developers to build responsive, emotional, and localized speech application

2026-04-06.name: Advancing Production ASR with 4-Bit Quantization and Domain Fine-Tuning
2026-04-06.description: In this episode, we explore the industrialization of the Whisper architecture through specialized optimization and edge deployment. We discuss how the VibeVoice-ASR-HF release utilizes NF4 quantization to reduce latency on consumer hardware while exploring domain-specific breakthroughs like the KerNeLGaming medical transcription model. The conversation also covers the rise of Small Speech Language Models like vak-v1 and how frameworks like Apple's MLX are enabling high-performance regional langu
